kicker: PT LEVEL I // UNITS & PROCESSING LINES

beats.narration: Every penetrant test runs the same sequence. What changes shop to shop is the hardware carrying parts through it — and it shows up two ways.

payload.title: UNITS & PROCESSING LINES
payload.sub: Same chemistry, two very different packages.

beats.narration: A stationary line strings the steps out as physical stations for baskets of parts. The whole line is the written procedure, built in steel.

payload.heading: The line is the procedure, in steel
payload.note: PRECLEAN → PENETRANT + DRAIN → WASH → DRY → DEVELOP → INSPECT

beats.narration: Each station controls one variable, and the developer sets the order. Aqueous goes on wet parts before the dryer; dry powder needs a dry surface.
The portable kit packs that chemistry into three aerosol cans — visible dye, Method C. No water, no dark booth. It goes where a line cannot.
The kit trades the line's built-in control for portability. The tanks and timers are gone — so the technician becomes the process control.

payload.sub: Learn each station and you can run either one.
